description: EP-AEC acceptance suite with REAL Ed25519 and Class-A WebAuthn signatures plus RP-scoped profiles, run cross-language through the built-in verifiers. Each vector: build the stub verifiers named in stub_types (permissive: valid iff evidence.valid !== false, digest = evidence.action_digest), run verifyAuthorizationChain with the vector requirement, expected action, verification time, and profiles, then compare allow to expect.valid.
algorithm: Ed25519 and Class-A WebAuthn over RFC 8785 (JCS) canonical bytes; RP-scoped acceptance profiles

vectors.description: A Section 6.2 Trust Receipt with a pinned Class-A WebAuthn approver, RP audience, policy, log key, and freshness profile verifies.
A validly signed receipt whose operator key is pinned for NO role is refused (fail closed without a role-scoped anchor).
CROSS-ROLE KEY CONFUSION: the machine key is pinned, but only for the policy_decision role. Its own signed object, relabeled EP-RECEIPT-v1 and binding this action, must not fill the human role.
A receipt validly signed over a DIFFERENT action, with only the unsigned top-level action_hash spoofed to this action, does not bind this action.
A policy_decision leg (a permissive stub) LABELED "ep-receipt" must not satisfy the registered ep-receipt requirement token.
CONTROL: a policy leg plus a fresh, profile-bound Class-A Trust Receipt satisfies "policy_decision AND ep-receipt".

FORGED QUORUM: an attacker builds an entire distinct-human quorum under device keys it generated. The ep-quorum leg fails closed because the relying party supplied no exact policy, RP ID, context policy, or enrolled approver directory.
A valid bundle evaluated only under the presenter requirement never produces relying-party allow.
The presenter's policy-plus-human bar passes, but the relying party also requires a quorum; the missing RP-required leg must deny.
Internal same-action agreement is not enough when the executor did not pin the action it will perform.
A valid bundle over one action is refused when the executor independently expects another action.
Presenter label cfo is display metadata and cannot create an authorization role.
A parser must consume the full requirement; ignored punctuation cannot turn a malformed bar into a valid one.
A custom verifier cannot replace the reserved ep-receipt built-in with a permissive policy stub.
A null component is a typed refusal, never a verifier crash.
A real 2-of-3 WebAuthn quorum is accepted only under the exact RP-pinned policy, audience, context policy, and approver directory.
A valid quorum document cannot downgrade the RP-pinned threshold by presenting a different policy.
Valid WebAuthn signatures for one RP are refused under a different relying-party audience.
A valid quorum is refused when its signed WebAuthn client origin is outside the relying-party allowlist.
A signed cross-origin quorum ceremony is refused even when the visible origin string is allowlisted.
A cryptographically valid historical quorum is refused after its signed authorization window and the RP max-age limit.
A valid quorum is refused when the relying-party approver registry snapshot is older than its pinned freshness limit.
A valid Class-A Trust Receipt is refused when evaluated after the pinned freshness limit.
A valid Class-A Trust Receipt is refused when its signed WebAuthn client origin is outside the relying-party allowlist.
A signed cross-origin Class-A receipt ceremony is refused even when the visible origin string is allowlisted.
A valid Ed25519 operator envelope cannot satisfy the human ep-receipt role without a Class-A ceremony.
A cryptographically valid Class-A receipt is refused when its RP-pinned directory entry is revoked.
A pinned key cannot be reassigned to a different approver identity by the presenter.
Safety-critical quorum admission requires distinct_humans true explicitly; omission cannot select a permissive default.
An unknown policy mode is refused rather than silently interpreted as threshold.
